content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

July 6, 1977

777-06-112

Honorable David R. Baty
Judge of the Municipal Court
Marin County Hall of Justice
San Rafael, CA 94903

Re: Opinion Request No. 77-011

Dear Judge Baty:

On June 30, 1977, you called me to obtain interim advice on whether you are required to disclose financial interests you inquired about in your opinion request. Specifically, you have indicated that you own stock in a pharmaceutical manufacturer whose products are sold at pharmacies throughout California, but which to your knowledge has no manufacturing or distribution outlets in California. You also own stock in a rubber company whose products are sold in California and which has a manufacturing plant in the state.

As you know, Government Code Section 82034 requires disclosure of investments in any business entity which "does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction, or has done business within the jurisdiction at any time during the two years prior to the time" your statement is due. The Commission accepted your opinion request in order to determine how the phrase "doing business" should be interpreted under this provision of the Act.

Until the Commission issues a final opinion, the staff advice is that "doing business" should be interpreted in the constitutional sense of minimum contacts. Under this standard, both the pharmaceutical company and the rubber company would be reportable. We shall advise you when your opinion request is scheduled for a hearing.

I also wish to advise you that at a recent meeting, the Commission directed the staff to accept for an opinion

23

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

Honorable David R. Baty
Page two
July 6, 1977


the fourth question posed in your letter of April 25, 1977.
That question concerned whether or not you are required to
disclose as an interest in real property your interest in a
deed of trust securing a promissory note. In preparing your
present statement of economic interest, you should, however,
follow the informal advice outlined in my letter to you of
May 10, 1977, in which I advised you that this interest was
reportable.

     If you have any questions concerning these matters,
please call me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Michael J. Baker
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

MJB:jo

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 827 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Enforcement Assistance     Audit Assistance      Executive Legal      Enforcement          Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662           322-5663             322-5671           322-0441            322-6444

December 13, 1978

Honorable David R. Baty
Judge of the Municipal Court
Marin County Hall of Justice
San Rafael, CA 94903

Re: Opinion Request No. 77-011

Dear Judge Baty:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation of
December 12, 1978, at which time you indicated that you
no longer require a formal opinion regarding the fourth
question posed in your opinion request of April 25, 1977.
That question concerned whether you are required to dis-
close as an interest in real property your interest in a
deed of trust securing a promissory note. You indicated
that you would continue to follow the advice provided to
you by Michael Baker in his letter of July 6, 1977, that
this interest is reportable.

Sincerely,

Lee C. Konathal
Chief
Legal Division

DAVID R. BATY
JUDGE OF THE MUNICIPAL COURT
MARIN COUNTY HALL OF JUSTICE
SAN RAFAEL, CALIFORNIA 94903

Request No. 661 011
Date Received 4/26/77
Response Due 5/10/77

April 25, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I would like an opinion, pursuant to §83114(a) of the
Government Code, as to whether the following securities are
investments within the meaning of §82034 of the Government
Code:

(1) The common stock of a pharmaceutical
manufacturer. The stock is registered with the
Securities and Exchange Commission and is traded
on the New York Stock Exchange. The company is
headquartered outside California, and has no
manufacturing or distribution facilities in this
jurisdiction. Its products are, however, sold
at retail in most of all the pharmacies in the
jurisdiction.

(2) The common stock of a rubber company. Same
facts as (1).

(3) The common stock of an oil company. Same
facts as (1). Its products are sold at two
service stations in the jurisdiction and possibly
at one or more auto supply stores.

(4) A promissory note executed by a husband and
wife, secured by a deed of trust to unimproved
real property, within the jurisdiction.

a. Are payments of principal and income
on the note required to be reported
under §87203?

If you have any published opinions relating to this
question, I would appreciate receiving a copy of those
opinions.

Very truly yours,

DAVID R. BATY

DRB/dr
